Cohort 1 Senior Fellows

Governor's Innovation Fellowship · February 2026

Program Overview

21Senior Fellows
9Agencies
15Departments
2026Cohort 1 · Inaugural Class

Established under Executive Order N-30-25 to modernize California state government through internal talent — practitioners embedded across agencies to drive measurable outcomes in efficiency, service delivery, and customer experience.

Health & Human Services Agency

Henri Aghaei Baradaran

Research Data Analyst, Research and Data Analytics Branch

Dept. of Developmental Services · Health & Human Services

  • Over a decade of data analysis experience across insurance, energy, and public sectors
  • Technical proficiency in SQL, Tableau, Power BI, and Python
  • Translates complex data into actionable insights for DDS programs serving 500,000+ Californians

Bryanna McAdams

Manager, Office of Strategic Planning — Program Innovation Unit

Dept. of Child Support Services · Health & Human Services

  • 13+ years providing child support services at county and state levels
  • Developed programs supporting military, incarcerated, and housing-insecure parents
  • Applies behavioral economics principles to enhance the customer experience

Kattya Trinh

All-Hazards Dashboard Product Owner & CalHHS GIS Administrator

Office of Technology & Solutions Integration · Health & Human Services

  • Led the All-Hazards Dashboard to national NASCIO recognition (2023)
  • Cloud-based real-time tool covering floods, wildfires, earthquakes, and power shutoffs
  • Background in emergency management, IT, and public sector innovation

Elizabeth Betancourt

Natural and Working Lands Policy Advisor

Natural Resources Agency · Natural Resources

Contracts & Procurement Services Branch Manager

Dept. of Water Resources · Natural Resources

  • Recognized throughout state government as a go-to procurement expert

Chief, Office of Training and Administrative Services

Legislative Director

Program Leadership

Amy Tong Senior Counselor to the Governor

30+ years of state service. Former Secretary of GovOps and State CIO. Leads efficiency and GenAI implementation across California government.

Stuart Drown Deputy Secretary, Government Operations Agency

Pioneer of agile procurement and human-centered design. Former Executive Director, Little Hoover Commission. Key figure in transforming CA's approach to IT modernization.

Anne Crew-Renzo Program Director, CalAcademy / ODI

Founded CalAcademy (2024), training 5,000+ state employees in innovation skills. 20+ years in education. Oversees Governor's Innovation Fellows program.
